definition: to distribute something fairly
sentence: Jane must apportion the pizza in a way that will satisfy all of her five children.

definition: a fortified high point in an ancient city
sentence: The acropolis of the ancient city had been preserved well.

definition: electric current causes chemical change
sentence: Using electric current to refine copper, the worker used electrolysis to complete the process.

definition: seeking pleasure and joy as life's priority
sentence: Some people criticize hedonism because they believe that people are selfish for giving themselves the things that delight them.

definition: a plant in the legume family
sentence: The fragrant melilot bloomed profusely throughout the sunny meadow.

definition: the sharp tip of a bird's beak
sentence: The ornithologist carefully examined the sharp tomium of the hawk's beak.

definition: a widespread destruction or devastation
sentence: His book tells of an environmental apocalypse.

definition: a sensible approach based on practical circumstances
sentence: Moderate politicians emphasize their pragmatism as compared to their more extreme counterparts.

definition: a descriptive word or phrase for a person
sentence: Gwen earned the epithet of "fastest woman on earth" after winning four gold medals in track in the last Olympics.

definition: sound patterns and rhythm in poetry
sentence: Dr. Seuss is famous for the prosody in his books, with rhyming lines and colorful sound patterns included in each work.

definition: an angle made by rotating a line around a point
sentence: The architect carefully calculated the perigon formed by the building's central tower.

definition: showing great care and effort
sentence: He has proven to be an assiduous student.

definition: deeply sad and lacking hope
sentence: Because I just won a huge lottery, I am no longer despondent about my financial concerns.

definition: an additional comment added at the end of correspondence
sentence: If you don't remember to include something in your letter until after it's written, you can use a postscript to add it in.

definition: showing no regret or remorse
sentence: Although he was really impenitent, the man pretended that he regretted stealing the check.

definition: a brief deviation from a usual course
sentence: If you do not follow the directions to the letter, you will experience a digression and possibly get lost.

definition: lacking direction or a clear aim
sentence: When I complain about the messy condition of my daughter's bedroom, she usually makes a desultory attempt to tidy up her space.

definition: to express something clearly and distinctly
sentence: Since a tour guide is required to speak to crowds of people, the ability to enunciate properly is a requirement for the position.

definition: to strongly and publicly scold someone
sentence: Because the president does not tolerate mistakes well, everyone knows he will castigate his staff members during their evening meeting.

definition: inherent beliefs or ideas present from birth
sentence: His moral compass was connate as he always seemed to know right from wrong.

definition: beneficial to health or well-being
sentence: Because of his religion, Hiram was a very salubrious eater who ate only vegetables and fruits.
